Name: Roman Aqueduct
Tips: 1st-century AD, 166 arches, 29m high — no mortar. Best viewed from Plaza del Azoguejo. Walk the full length. Morning light best.

Name: Alcázar de Segovia
Address: Plaza Reina Victoria Eugenia s/n Segovia
Tips: Disney's Cinderella castle inspiration. Perched on a cliff at confluence of two rivers. Climb Torre de Juan II (148 steps) for 360° panorama.

Name: Segovia Cathedral
Tips: Last great Gothic cathedral built in Spain (1525). Beautiful carved choir stalls and cloister. Quieter than Toledo cathedral.

Name: Jewish Quarter (La Judería)
Tips: Medieval Jewish quarter between aqueduct and cathedral. One of Spain's best-preserved. Corpus Christi convent built on the old synagogue site.

Name: Vera Cruz Church
Tips: 12th-century Templar church with rare 12-sided design. Climb the tower for Alcázar views. 15-min walk from old city — almost no tourists.

Name: Mirador del Alcázar (valley view)
Tips: Walk down from the Alcázar to the Eresma river valley for the classic cliff-castle view. The postcard shot of Segovia. Worth the descent.

Name: Casa de la Moneda (Royal Mint)
Tips: Spain's first royal mint on the banks of the Eresma. Interesting industrial history. Almost no tourists.

Name: Plaza Mayor
Tips: Segovia's main square — quieter and more beautiful than most. Cathedral on one side, cafés on the others. Good for ponche segoviano and coffee.
